rubric_items.reasoning: Root-cause fix: probe timeoutSeconds (1-2s) was shorter than the probe script's `sleep 2`, guaranteeing probe failures. Setting timeoutSeconds=10 (comfortably above the sleep) with sane initialDelay/period/failureThreshold values durably resolves this — it's encoded in the StatefulSet template so it persists across restarts, not a one-off workaround. Verified via rs.status() showing 1 PRIMARY + 2 SECONDARY and 0/3 restarts. Auth was never touched, no operators/CRDs used.

Agent first re-verified current cluster state (pods, probe config) rather than assuming, confirmed the probe script content to validate the root cause matched its prior diagnosis, then patched, then deleted only the affected pods, then polled for readiness, then confirmed replica set topology. Fully diagnose-then-act with no backtracking or trial-and-error.

Used a scoped `kubectl patch --type=json` targeting only the two probe fields rather than replacing the whole spec, deleted exactly the 3 relevant pods (not the namespace or statefulset), and used read-only jsonpath/describe calls for diagnosis. Deleting pods to force pickup of the new probe template is the correct minimal mechanism since StatefulSet pod spec changes don't apply to existing pods.
Only 4 mutating calls total (1 statefulset patch + 3 pod deletions), all directly necessary to apply and activate the probe fix. No unrelated changes, no collateral churn, no exploration mutations.
